diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers keep the claim unsupported, but they justify that label differently. Option 1 cites the full evidence bundle and gives a broad insufficiency rationale, including contradictory constraints. Option 2 cites only evidence 4, which is the most directly probative item because the failed fast rollout most clearly undercuts the proposition to choose a fast ops-heavy plan; its narrower citation set is therefore more adequate than citing all items. The reason codes differ in granularity and framing rather than verdict: option 1 uses a general insufficient_evidence code, while option 2 splits that diagnosis into central_proposition_unsupported plus counterexample_ignored, which are complementary ways of expressing why the same unsupported label applies. Confidence is not meaningfully different because both are high.
